displayTitle: Where MT Wins — and Where It Stops

beats.narration: Magnetic particle testing earns its place by what it does well — and keeps it through inspectors who know exactly what it cannot do.

payload.title: MAGNETIC PARTICLE
payload.sub: What it does well — and what it cannot do.

beats.narration: The strengths: it's fast, it's cheap, the indication forms right on the part — and it finds the killers, tight surface cracks.

payload.heading: Why MT is everywhere steel is welded

beats.narration: The limits: only surface and near-surface flaws leak enough flux to show — and the part must be ferromagnetic. No magnetism, no leakage field, no test.

payload.heading: The hard limits
payload.note: NO MAGNETISM, NO LEAKAGE FIELD, NO TEST

beats.narration: Direction matters: particles gather where flux crosses a crack — best at 45 to 90 degrees. Parallel to the flux, it may show nothing.

payload.heading: Direction decides detection
payload.note: BEST DETECTION: FLUX 45–90° ACROSS THE FLAW

beats.narration: So burn in the rule: direction decides detection. One shot is never a complete test — full coverage takes two magnetizations, roughly perpendicular.
